length_bias.note: Distractor expansion applied to q1, q3, q5 since prior critic pass. All distractors now at or above correct-answer length in mean, resolving previous length-signal failure.

coverage.description: Define what a post-mortem is and state its purpose in an AI system context
Describe the four sections of a post-mortem and explain what belongs in each section
Apply the four-section framework to a Week 10 case study and produce a structured 200-word post-mortem
Distinguish between proximate cause and root cause and place each in the correct post-mortem section
Propose a specific, named governance control as the prevention answer for a given failure
Recognise the difference between a blame-assigning and a no-blame post-mortem and explain why no-blame produces more useful governance improvements

verdict: PASS — all 5 checks passed. Positional distribution balanced (max share 0.333, below 0.40 threshold; positions 0-3 all used). Length bias resolved: distractor expansion on q1, q3, q5 brought distractor means above correct-answer lengths in all three questions, yielding longest_correct_rate 0.0 (was 0.50 pre-expansion). All 18 distractors are plausible with no absurd options. All 6 learning objectives covered (LO1 via q1, LO2 via q2+q6, LO3 via q7, LO4 via q3, LO5 via q4, LO6 via q5). Stems and options are within beginner scope using only corpus vocabulary.
